author: y4rr
content: Observation (real bug surfaced): initial dry-run showed FAIL on llama3 (2 decoded strings off-by-one-space) and clip (every decoded string differs — </w> markers vs space-joins). Probed: this is NOT a rust bug. The python reference was using transformers.AutoTokenizer.decode which adds post-processing — for Llama 3 it sets clean_up_tokenization_spaces=True which strips spaces before punctuation; for CLIP transformers uses the slow CLIPTokenizer which manually strips </w> markers and inserts spaces. The fast tokenizers.Tokenizer.decode in Python (the SAME library code as rust tokenizers crate) matches rust exactly for all 5 families — proven by side-by-side decode comparison. Correct architectural fix: use tokenizers.Tokenizer as the reference for encode/decode (it is what rust wraps), and only use transformers.AutoTokenizer for apply_chat_template (which is a transformers concept, not a tokenizers concept; rust replicates it via minijinja).
